Title: Disruption of the rice 4-DEOXYOROBANCHOL HYDROXYLASE unravels specific functions of canonical strigolactones
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizospheric signal mediating arbuscular mycorrhizal symbiosis and stimulating parasitic weed seed germination; contributes to rice root, shoot, and panicle developmental regulation indirectly through canonical strigolactone balance.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Canonical strigolactone branch; possible crosstalk with noncanonical 4-oxo-MeCLA route
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream, terminal hydroxylation step from 4DO to Oro
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 4-deoxyorobanchol to form orobanchol in rice; data also suggest involvement in conversion of 4-oxo-MeCLA.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of canonical strigolactone composition; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-associated context under normal and low-Pi conditions; transcriptome analyzed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low phosphate strongly used for SL quantification; normal vs low-Pi conditions; exogenous 4DO treatment; TIS108 inhibitor treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of root tissues and exudates; double-mutant analysis; chemical inhibition with TIS108; exogenous 4DO application; phenotyping; mycorrhization and Striga germination assays; RNA-seq; hormone quantification
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of OsMAX1-1400 abolishes orobanchol and causes 4DO accumulation; accumulated 4DO shortens shoot height, panicle length, and panicle base length; disruption negatively affects mycorrhization and alters rhizospheric signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR disruption of OsMAX1-1400 caused complete absence of orobanchol in rice roots and exudates. 2. OsMAX1-1400 loss led to accumulation of the precursor 4DO and elevated 4-oxo-MeCLA, confirming OsMAX1-1400 as the rice 4DO hydroxylase and suggesting additional involvement in noncanonical SL conversion. 3. Os1400 mutants displayed reduced plant height, panicle length, and panicle base length without a tillering phenotype. 4. The Os900/1400 double mutant lacking both 4DO and Oro did not show the Os1400 architectural defects, demonstrating that these defects are due to 4DO accumulation rather than Oro deficiency. 5. OsMAX1-1400 disruption reduced optimal AM fungal colonization and altered Striga-related rhizospheric communication.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: NCBI GEO GSE221837; metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing OsMAX1-1400 alone is not ideal because it causes 4DO accumulation-associated architectural defects and reduced mycorrhization. However, understanding this step helps separate canonical SL functions and may inform combinatorial engineering with upstream MAX1 genes to reshape rhizospheric signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height and panicle traits; impaired AM colonization; altered auxin homeostasis due to 4DO accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsMAX1-1400 knockout did not increase tillering in Nipponbare, indicating canonical SLs are not major tillering inhibitors in this background. Data suggest pathway interference between canonical and noncanonical SL biosynthesis and possible conversion of 4-oxo-MeCLA by OsMAX1-1400.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizospheric signal for parasitic weed germination and AM fungal communication; component of canonical strigolactone blend in rice.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Canonical strigolactone branch; contributes to noncanonical 4-oxo-MeCLA route
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream step converting carlactone-derived intermediates to 4DO
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: carlactone (CL) via repeated oxygenation and ring closure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms 4-deoxyorobanchol from carlactone through repeated oxygenation and ring closure; also contributes to formation of 4-oxo-MeCLA from CL+30 with a putative methyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-forming step for canonical SL production; not explicitly established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-associated context under low-Pi SL analysis
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low phosphate; double-mutant context with OsMAX1-1400; TIS108 inhibits OsMAX1-900 and OsMAX1-1400
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant comparison (Os900 single and Os900/1400 double mutants); LC-MS/MS quantification of root and exudate strigolactones; Striga germination and mycorrhization assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsMAX1-900 is required for 4DO and Oro production; loss eliminates canonical SLs but does not cause high tillering in Nipponbare, while reducing Striga germination activity and delaying mycorrhization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMAX1-900 is the upstream canonical SL biosynthetic enzyme producing 4DO from carlactone. 2. Os900 mutants lacked both 4DO and Oro in roots and exudates. 3. Os900/1400 double mutant analysis showed that removal of OsMAX1-900 suppresses the Os1400 growth defects by preventing 4DO accumulation. 4. Os900 contributes strongly to Striga germination activity and early mycorrhization through canonical SL production. 5. Residual 4-oxo-MeCLA in Os900 mutants indicates a minor Os900-independent route for this noncanonical SL.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target to reduce canonical SL-mediated Striga germination while largely preserving rice architecture in Nipponbare; double manipulation with OsMAX1-1400 can avoid 4DO accumulation-associated penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Delay in mycorrhization and altered rhizospheric communication due to reduced canonical SL exudation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In this study OsMAX1-900 is a core pathway comparator because the double mutant was essential to prove that Os1400 phenotypes derive from 4DO accumulation.
